description: Fix CLI UX friction found during fresh-user testing: (1) every error message and some normal outputs print twice due to Commander.js async action handling, (2) inconsistent browser-tool naming in config set help examples. LOW issues noted but not addressed: plugin search shows blank description for slack (npm metadata, not a CLI bug); plugin list when server is down says 'No plugins installed' without noting server is offline.

userStories.title: Fix duplicated CLI output on all error and some success paths
userStories.description: As a user, I want every CLI message to print exactly once so that I can clearly read and act on the output without confusion.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs (no args, server not running) prints 'Server not running' message exactly once
opentabs strat (unknown command) prints error and help output exactly once
opentabs status (server not running) prints error message exactly once
opentabs logs (no log file) prints error message exactly once
opentabs config set invalidkey foo prints error message exactly once
opentabs config set prot 9515 prints 'Did you mean port?' error exactly once
opentabs config set port notanumber prints validation error exactly once
opentabs config reset (without --confirm) prints confirmation prompt exactly once
opentabs config rotate-secret (without --confirm) prints confirmation prompt exactly once
opentabs plugin remove slack (without --confirm) prints confirmation prompt exactly once
opentabs plugin install nonexistent-plugin prints error exactly once
opentabs start (port already in use) prints port conflict error exactly once
opentabs audit (server not running) prints error message exactly once
All error commands still exit with code 1
All success commands still produce correct output
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Root cause: In platform/cli/src/cli.ts, all .action() handlers return async promises (the handler functions like handleStatus, handleStart, etc. are async). When a handler calls console.error() + process.exit(1), the promise rejects. Commander.js catches the rejected promise and re-prints the error/output because .showHelpAfterError() and .showSuggestionAfterError() are configured on line 28-29.

The fix should be in platform/cli/src/cli.ts. The approach: wrap the action callbacks so that rejected promises are caught and handled without Commander.js re-printing them. One approach is to use Commander's .exitOverride() so that when a command handler calls process.exit(), Commander throws a CommanderError instead of exiting — then catch that in a wrapper. Another approach: wrap each .action() callback in a helper that catches errors and calls process.exit(1) in a way that doesn't leave a rejected promise for Commander to catch (e.g., catch the promise rejection and exit cleanly).

The simplest correct fix: add a wrapper function like `function wrapAction(fn) { return (...args) => fn(...args).catch(() => {}) }` that swallows the rejection since the handler already called process.exit(1). Apply this wrapper to the .action() callback on line 37-44 of cli.ts. Then update each registerXxxCommand function to wrap its .action() callback similarly.

Affected files:
- platform/cli/src/cli.ts (line 37-44: default action, line 56: parseAsync)
- platform/cli/src/commands/status.ts (line 236: .action())
- platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts (look for .action())
- platform/cli/src/commands/stop.ts (look for .action())
- platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts (multiple .action() calls for set, show, reset, rotate-secret)
- platform/cli/src/commands/logs.ts (look for .action())
- platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts (look for .action())
- platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts (multiple .action() calls for search, list, install, remove, create)
- platform/cli/src/commands/audit.ts (look for .action())
- platform/cli/src/commands/update.ts (look for .action())

Alternative simpler approach: define a wrapAction helper in cli.ts and export it, then import in each command file. Or even simpler: just add .exitOverride() to the program in cli.ts and handle the CommanderError in a try-catch around program.parseAsync(). Test by running `opentabs status` with no server and verifying output appears only once.

userStories.title: Fix inconsistent browser-tool naming in config set help examples
userStories.description: As a user, I want the config set help examples to use consistent naming so that I know which form of tool name to use.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs config set --help shows browser-tool examples using the same naming convention (both use browser_ prefixed canonical names)
The example on line 671 uses browser-tool.browser_execute_script (matching line 672 style) instead of browser-tool.execute_script
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Simple one-line fix in platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts line 671.

Change:
  $ opentabs config set browser-tool.execute_script disabled
To:
  $ opentabs config set browser-tool.browser_execute_script disabled

This makes it consistent with line 672 which already uses browser-tool.browser_execute_script.
